issues.description: The new toolCall object reads `msg.metadata || {}`, but the `ToolCall` outbound message type in gui/sidecar/src/types.ts (lines 40-45) has no `metadata` field — only `type`, `id`, `tool_name`, `tool_input`. This is a TypeScript compile error. It also adds a `metadata` property to the stored tool call, but `ToolCallInfo` (claudeStore.ts lines 10-16) has no `metadata` field either, so the push of `toolCall` into `m.toolCalls` will also fail type checking.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) drop the `metadata` field entirely since the sidecar doesn't emit it, or (b) extend both the `ToolCall` outbound type in sidecar/src/types.ts AND the `ToolCallInfo` interface to include `metadata?: Record<string, unknown>` — and justify the addition with a real consumer.

issues.description: The resultCache entry reads `msg.tool_call_id`, `msg.output`, and `msg.is_error`, none of which exist on the `ToolResult` outbound message type (sidecar/src/types.ts lines 47-52 defines only `type`, `id`, `tool_name`, `result`). All three accesses are TypeScript errors and all three will be `undefined` at runtime, so `resultCache[undefined] = { output: undefined, is_error: undefined }` — the cache is populated with a single garbage key.
issues.suggested_fix: Use the fields that actually exist on ToolResult (`msg.result`, keyed by something real such as `msg.tool_name` or the matched tool call id from `tc.id`). If tool_call_id/output/is_error are intended as a new wire format, update the `ToolResult` interface and the sidecar emitter together.

issues.description: `AssistantMessage` (claudeStore.ts lines 18-27) has no `resultCache` field. Assigning `m.resultCache = resultCache` inside the `produce` callback where `m` is typed `ChatMessage`/`AssistantMessage` is a TypeScript error. There is no `as any` on this line to silence it, unlike the tool_call path.
issues.suggested_fix: Either delete the resultCache mechanism (preferred — see dead-code finding) or add `resultCache?: Record<string, { output: unknown; is_error: boolean }>` to `AssistantMessage` and initialize it in `sendMessage` alongside `toolCalls: []`.

issues.description: ID generation was moved out of the `produce` callback: `id: `${msg.id}-tc-${state.messages[idx]?.role === 'assistant' ? (state.messages[idx] as any).toolCalls.length : 0}`. This reads the store via `state.messages[idx]` and casts to `any`, bypassing the properly-typed `m` parameter that `produce` already provides. The original `m.toolCalls.length` inside produce was type-safe, concise, and guaranteed to observe the current draft. The new form adds `as any`, duplicates the role check that `findAssistantIdx` already guarantees, and needlessly reads reactive state outside the mutation scope. In the two-assistant-message edge case (if the store ever reorders), reading by idx outside produce vs. inside is also subtly more fragile.
issues.suggested_fix: Revert to computing the id inside produce using `m.toolCalls.length` (already type-safe with the `m.role !== 'assistant'` narrowing). No `as any` needed.

issues.description: `resultCache` has zero consumers anywhere in `gui/` (grep for `resultCache` returns nothing outside this diff). The same data is already tracked on the tool call itself (`tc.status = 'complete'; tc.result = msg.result`), so the cache duplicates information the store already holds. It adds a second, parallel representation of tool results that can drift from `toolCalls[i].result`, plus code, plus allocations, for no observed benefit.
issues.suggested_fix: Delete the entire resultCache block. Readers who need to look up a result by id can iterate `m.toolCalls` (constant-size per message) or add a tiny helper `findResult(m, id)`. Only introduce a cache if/when a profiled hot path needs it and a consumer exists.

issues.description: Even if the cache were kept, building it by shallow-cloning the previous cache on every tool_result (`{ ...(state.messages[idx] as any).resultCache || {} }`) is O(n) per insert, so handling N tool results is O(N²) allocations and copies. Since the mutation is already happening inside a `produce` draft, the immutable spread is pointless — produce handles structural sharing.
issues.suggested_fix: If kept, initialize `m.resultCache ??= {}` inside the produce callback and then do `m.resultCache[key] = { output, is_error }` — one assignment, no spread, no `as any`.

issues.description: No tests are added or updated. The diff changes two of the four outbound message handlers (tool_call id formation, tool_result bookkeeping) and introduces a new field on assistant messages. There is no assertion that (a) tool call ids remain stable/unique, (b) resultCache is populated with the correct key/value, or (c) the existing pending-tool-call completion logic in tool_result still works after the refactor. Store behavior on the streaming path is exactly the kind of thing a unit test in `claudeStore.test.ts` (or equivalent) should cover.
issues.suggested_fix: Add tests that drive `handleOutboundMessage` with a synthetic sequence (tool_call → tool_result → done) and assert the resulting message tree shape. If resultCache stays, assert its contents match the expected keys/values.

issues.description: Both new code paths guard with `state.messages[idx]?.role === 'assistant' ? ... : {}`, but `findAssistantIdx` already returned early on non-assistant, and `idx === -1` is already handled. The ternary branch is unreachable and obscures intent — it also forces the `as any` cast because TS can't narrow outside produce. Inside produce the existing `if (m.role !== 'assistant') return;` narrowing is the canonical place to do this check.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the outside-produce ternary; perform all reads and writes inside the produce callback using the properly-narrowed `m`.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: This diff is not mergeable as written. The tool_result handler reads three fields (`tool_call_id`, `output`, `is_error`) that do not exist on `ToolResult` in `gui/sidecar/src/types.ts`, and the tool_call handler reads `msg.metadata` which also doesn't exist — these are TypeScript errors and, at runtime, populate the new `resultCache` with an `undefined` key and undefined values. Separately, `m.resultCache = ...` assigns to a field that isn't declared on `AssistantMessage`, another compile error. Beyond the type breakage, the `resultCache` itself is dead weight: it duplicates data already tracked on `toolCalls[i].result`, has zero consumers in `gui/`, and is constructed with an O(n²) spread on every update. The tool_call id refactor is a pure regression — it moves a trivially type-safe `m.toolCalls.length` out of `produce` just to add an `as any` cast and a redundant role check. Recommended path: delete the `resultCache` mechanism entirely, revert the tool_call id computation to the original form, and — if a metadata field is genuinely needed — extend `ToolCall`/`ToolCallInfo` together and add tests for the streaming store behavior.
